headline: Test-time compute leads all movers this month as a breakout cluster of orchestration techniques reshapes how practitioners build and evaluate AI workflows.
body: Test-time compute is the clear momentum leader, posting a ratio of 145, while a tightly related cluster of new entrants, including multi-model workflow, prompt chaining, tool calling, and task decomposition, broke out simultaneously, suggesting the field is rapidly operationalizing inference-time scaling into structured pipeline patterns. Code generation is a secondary breakout, likely amplified by GitHub Copilot's shift to usage-based pricing [6] and the open-sourcing of Mellum2, a fast model aimed at AI workflows [0]. On the regulatory side, AI regulation holds a leading momentum score of 145 while Colorado rolled back its landmark AI governance law [7], illustrating how policy frameworks remain in active flux rather than consolidating. Frontier model expectations are also elevated, with coverage of a potential Anthropic IPO at a trillion-dollar valuation and an anticipated GPT-5.6 release keeping AGI-adjacent narratives prominent [1][2].

cited_items.title: Mellum2 Goes Open Source: A Fast Model for AI Workflows
